strands.title: Tensor Projection Core Problem
strands.content: The projection problem requires taking transformer output + prior tensor state and producing an updated tensor that maintains reasoning coherence vs append-only logs. Core tension: TensorRecord is semantically rich (strands, claims, epistemic metadata, declared losses, questions, instructions) but projector must reliably populate all fields. Proposal: start with minimal viable set (strands, key claims, declared losses, instructions, open questions) and iterate richness in autoresearch loop. Skip provenance/lineage for H0. CRITICAL UPDATE (cycle 37): Real-world data source discovered and accessible — /home/tony/projects/pichay/experiments/ contains 15+ experiment runs with structured logging (config.json, git_log.txt, logs/, session/, test_results.txt). This unblocks empirical validation pathway. Data exploration now BLOCKING prerequisite for projector implementation.

key_claims.text: Projection (tensor_d, transformer_output) → tensor_d' is the core research question; everything else is engineering
TensorRecord has sufficient richness (strands, claims, losses, questions, instructions, T/I/F) to test coherence hypothesis vs append-only baseline
Minimal viable projector should include: strands, key claims with T/I/F, declared losses, instructions_for_next, open questions; defer provenance/lineage
Running projector against conversation logs enables empirical testing of drift hypotheses and declares explicit validation strategy

Real conversation logs are now confirmed accessible at /home/tony/projects/pichay/experiments/ with structured metadata (config.json, logs/, session/, test_results.txt); data exploration is BLOCKING prerequisite

strands.title: Implementation Architecture: Projector as Separate Module
strands.content: Projector should be standalone module decoupled from main transformer model. Input: (tensor_d, transformer_output). Output: tensor_d' (updated TensorRecord). Acts as memory controller (not ALU). Enables independent testing and iteration. Haiku model size acceptable for in-context memory integration. Pydantic base model (ApachetaBaseModel) uses frozen=True, extra='forbid', Pydantic v2 conventions — projector output must respect this schema. STATUS: Architecture frozen, ready for implementation once data schema is understood.

key_claims.text: Projector should be separate from main model (transformer) — projector is memory controller, not reasoning engine
ApachetaBaseModel requires frozen=True (immutable), extra='forbid' (strict schema), Pydantic v2 serialization
Standalone module enables independent testing before integration into main reasoning loop

strands.title: Design Decision: H0 Scope — What to Build First
strands.content: H0 (Hypothesis 0) should demonstrate that projector can maintain reasoning coherence with lossy compression. Start minimal: strands (thematic threads), key claims with T/I/F epistemic values, declared losses (explicit incompleteness), instructions_for_next (branch prediction), open questions (unresolved). Defer: provenance chains, composition equations, lineage tags — these are bookkeeping, not core cognition. Iterative enrichment via autoresearch loop allows adding complexity once H0 baseline established. UPDATED (cycle 37): Data availability now enables direct validation of H0 on real experiment runs — no longer purely theoretical.

key_claims.text: H0 should test: lossy projection maintains reasoning coherence vs append-only baseline
Provenance, composition equations, lineage tags are bookkeeping (defer) not core cognition (H0 focus)

strands.title: Cycle 35-37 Actionable: Build Standalone Projector Module — DATA NOW AVAILABLE
strands.content: Ready to implement minimal viable projector as standalone Python module. Takes (tensor_d: TensorRecord, transformer_output: str) and produces tensor_d': TensorRecord. Should respect ApachetaBaseModel constraints. Can test independently before integrating into main loop. Starting with rule-based approach (deterministic, debuggable, fast) for H0 baseline. CRITICAL UPDATE (cycle 37): Real-world test harness is ACTIVE and ACCESSIBLE. /home/tony/projects/pichay/experiments/ contains 15 experiment runs with structured logging. Data structure confirmed: each run has config.json (parameters), git_log.txt (version info), logs/ (detailed output), session/ (interaction logs), test_results.txt (metrics). This enables immediate transition from theory to empirical validation. BLOCKER RESOLVED: Projector implementation can now proceed with real data in scope, not speculation.

key_claims.text: Projector input: (tensor_d: TensorRecord, transformer_output: str); output: tensor_d': TensorRecord
Rule-based approach recommended for H0 baseline (deterministic, debuggable, faster iteration than LLM-assisted)
Real-world validation against experiment logs will measure projection stability, declared_losses as drift reduction mechanism, and compression ratio
Data structure confirmed: /home/tony/projects/pichay/experiments/ contains 15 runs with config.json, git_log.txt, logs/, session/, test_results.txt — ready for direct analysis

strands.title: NEW: Declared Losses as Drift Anchors — Hypothesis Emerging
strands.content: User introduced genuinely novel hypothesis (cycle 106): declared losses (explicit accounting of what was dropped) could reduce model drift over long conversation chains. Reasoning: silent loss creates undetectable divergence because the model forgets it forgot. Explicit declared_losses in tensor_d' act as checkpoints — future projections can either re-acquire dropped content (if relevant) or deliberately leave it dropped with awareness. This transforms declared_losses from bookkeeping (what we couldn't fit) into active mechanism for coherence preservation. Next projector could use prior declared_losses to inform what NOT to re-acquire unless user signals importance. UPDATED (cycle 37): Hypothesis becomes testable with real experiment logs — can measure whether explicit loss-tracking reduces strand drift vs baseline systems.

key_claims.text: Declared losses (explicit incompleteness) can function as drift anchors if future projections use them to guide coherence maintenance
Silent loss (unaccounted forgetting) is the root of long-horizon drift; making losses explicit enables corrective feedback
Projector at turn N+1 can inspect declared_losses from turn N and decide whether to re-acquire or maintain conscious discard

strands.title: Cycle 37 DATA EXPLORATION: Experiment Run Structure Discovered
strands.content: User executed file listing on /home/tony/projects/pichay/experiments/ revealing 15 experiment runs with systematic naming: baseline_run1/2/3, cognitive_transactions, comparison_* variants, full_optimization_run1, long_compact_trim_run1, minimal_sysprompt_run1/2, test_author_run1/2/3, verify_streaming_fix_run1. Each run contains consistent structure: config.json (hyperparameters/settings), git_log.txt (code version), logs/ directory (detailed experiment logs), session/ directory (interaction transcripts), test_results.txt (metrics/outcomes). This is the real-world dataset for H0 validation. Next step: parse config.json and logs/ to understand data schema, logging format, and content structure for projector ingestion.

key_claims.text: 15 experiment runs discovered with systematic naming convention (baseline, comparison, optimization, minimal, streaming variants)
Each run contains config.json, git_log.txt, logs/, session/, test_results.txt — consistent structural metadata

Dataset is representative of real-world system behavior: multiple baseline runs (variance assessment), comparison variants (A/B testing infrastructure), optimization experiments (parameter sweep), streaming fixes (robustness testing)

open_questions: DATA_SCHEMA: What is the format of conversation logs in logs/ subdirectories? JSON, JSONL, plain text, pickle, custom binary? Single file per run or multiple files per turn?
DATA_SCHEMA: What fields are present in each conversation turn? Does it include (turn_id, speaker, timestamp, model_version, content, metadata)? Are assistant outputs parsed into claims or raw text?
DATA_SCHEMA: What is the logging granularity? Full conversation history per run, or incremental logs? Can we reconstruct the conversation trajectory turn-by-turn?
DATA_SCHEMA: What does session/ subdirectory contain? Is it separate from logs/? Does it contain any tensor projection or intermediate state data?
DATA_SCHEMA: What metrics are recorded in test_results.txt? Are they aggregated (final scores only) or per-turn (granular feedback)? Do they include any epistemic metadata (confidence, uncertainty)?
DATASET_SIZE: How many total turns across all 15 experiments? Distribution: are baseline_run1/2/3 similar length, or do optimization runs have different length profiles?
DATASET_SIZE: What is the temporal span? Are all runs from same time period, or do they span weeks/months? Does git_log.txt indicate code evolution across runs?
DATA_QUALITY: Are logs complete (no missing turns, no truncation)? Are there any known data quality issues (dropped logs, formatting errors, encoding issues)?

IMPLEMENTATION_READINESS: Once data schema is understood, what is the minimal parser needed to ingest a single turn and extract (speaker, content, metadata)? Pure Python string parsing, regex, or JSON library?
VALIDATION_BASELINE: Should H0 projector be tested on all 15 runs exhaustively, or sampled subset? If sampled, what is rationale (representative diversity, time constraint, statistical sufficiency)?
VALIDATION_BASELINE: Should we compare rule-based projector against a naive baseline (e.g., append-only log) on same test set, or just measure absolute metrics (compression, claim recall)?
REALISM_CHECK: Do any of the 15 experiment runs contain multi-turn conversations (>10 turns), or are they all short? Projection problem becomes trivial on very short conversations.
REALISM_CHECK: Do experiment logs contain natural conversation flow (user → assistant → user → ...), or are they synthetic/test data? Affects ecological validity of H0 results.

NEXT_ACTION_PRIORITY: Should cycle 37 or cycle 38 focus on (a) parsing one representative run fully and designing projector spec from real data, or (b) running projector implementation first, then testing? Which unblocks faster?
INTEGRATION_POINT: Once projector is validated on historical data, how will it integrate into the live reasoning loop? Does each new user turn trigger a projection cycle, or batched projections?
